name: BasicTheories.ListTheory

values.name: head
values.comment: Safely get a head of a non-empty list

values.name: lengthOfNonEmptyList
values.comment: Get the length of a non-empty list
values.type: RefinementProofs.Proven (List.List a) BasicTheories.ListTheory.NonEmptyList -> RefinementProofs.Proven Basics.Int BasicTheories.NumberTheory.Positive

values.name: mkSortedList
values.comment: Make a sorted list.

values.name: nonEmptyListMap
values.comment: Map over a non-empty list
values.type: (a -> b) -> RefinementProofs.Proven (List.List a) BasicTheories.ListTheory.NonEmptyList -> RefinementProofs.Proven (List.List b) BasicTheories.ListTheory.NonEmptyList

values.name: proveNonEmptyList
values.comment: Prove that a list is non-empty

name: BasicTheories.NumberTheory
comment:
Some basic number proofs

# Definition
@docs Even
    , Negative
    , Odd
    , Positive
    , Zero
    , ZeroOrGreater

# Proofs
@docs proveEven
    , proveNegative
    , proveOdd
    , provePositive
    , proveZeroOrGreater

# Implications
@docs allPositivesAreZeroOrGreater

aliases.name: Negative
aliases.comment: A negative number. Alias for `Not ZeroOrGreater`

aliases.name: ZeroOrGreater
aliases.comment: type alias for `Or Zero Positive`. If used with an Int this the number is 0,1,2,3,....

values.name: allPositivesAreZeroOrGreater
values.comment:
A simple implication.
    Note this is strictly not needed since this implication is clear in the types - `ZeroOrGreater = Or Zero Positive`.
    An alternative is to use `introOrL` or `or`.

values.name: proveEven
values.comment: Prove that a number is even

values.name: proveNegative
values.comment: Prove a number is negative

values.name: proveOdd
values.comment: Prove that a number is odd

values.name: provePositive
values.comment: Prove that a number is positive (>0)

values.name: proveZeroOrGreater
values.comment: Prove that a number is either zero or greater

name: BasicTheories.StringTheory
comment:
Some basic string proofs

# Definition
@docs NonEmptyString
    , TrimmedString
    , NonEmptyTrimmedString

# Proofs
@docs mkTrimmedString
    , proveNonEmptyString
    , proveNonEmptyTrimmedString
    , proveTrimmedString

# Functions
@docs lengthOfNonEmptyString

aliases.name: NonEmptyTrimmedString
aliases.comment: A string that are both nonEmpty and trimmed, alias for "And NonEmptyString TrimmedString"

values.name: lengthOfNonEmptyString
values.comment: Get the length of a non-empty string
values.type: RefinementProofs.Proven String.String BasicTheories.StringTheory.NonEmptyString -> RefinementProofs.Proven Basics.Int BasicTheories.NumberTheory.Positive

values.name: mkTrimmedString
values.comment: Make a trimmed string from a string. Note: will trim a non-trimmed string

values.name: proveNonEmptyString
values.comment: Prove that a string is non-empty

values.name: proveNonEmptyTrimmedString
values.comment: Prove that a string is both non-empty and trimmed

values.name: proveTrimmedString
values.comment: Prove that a string is trimmed

name: RefinementProofs

unions.name: Implies
unions.comment: Implies that if "p1" holds than "p2" holds

unions.name: Proven
unions.comment: A value "a" that is proven to hold the property "b"

values.name: and
values.comment: Used by library writers to create build a And proof

values.name: axiom
values.comment:
Used by library writers to create axioms with non-exported constructors.
    Remember, the library/module constructors must not be exported!

values.name: elimAndL
values.comment: Remove the left hand side of an And proof
values.type: RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.And b c) -> RefinementProofs.Proven a b

values.name: elimAndR
values.comment: Remove the right hand side of an And proof
values.type: RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.And b c) -> RefinementProofs.Proven a c

values.name: exorcise
values.comment: Extract the actual value and remove the proof. This is always safe and a library writer should always expect and allow its users to do this.

values.name: imply
values.comment: Used by library writers to imply proof relations

values.name: introOrL
values.comment: Same as introOrR but introduces the p2 on the other side
values.type: RefinementProofs.Proven a p1 -> RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.Or p2 p1)

values.name: introOrR
values.comment: If a is proven to hold property p1 then classic logic gives that a holds either p1 or p2
values.type: RefinementProofs.Proven a p1 -> RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.Or p1 p2)

values.name: inverse
values.comment: Used by library writers to show that when `p1` does not hold for a then `Not p1` holds.
values.type: p1 -> (a -> Maybe.Maybe (RefinementProofs.Proven a p1)) -> a -> Maybe.Maybe (RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.Not p1))

values.name: makeAnd
values.comment: A convience method to check two proofs at once for And
values.type: (a -> Maybe.Maybe (RefinementProofs.Proven a b)) -> (a -> Maybe.Maybe (RefinementProofs.Proven a c)) -> a -> Maybe.Maybe (RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.And b c))

values.name: makeOr
values.comment: A convience method to check two proofs for Or
values.type: (a -> Maybe.Maybe (RefinementProofs.Proven a b)) -> (a -> Maybe.Maybe (RefinementProofs.Proven a c)) -> a -> Maybe.Maybe (RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.Or b c))

values.name: modusPonens
values.comment: If a library implies that when p1 holds the p2 holds then you have proven p2 by prove p1
values.type: RefinementProofs.Implies p1 p2 -> RefinementProofs.Proven a p1 -> RefinementProofs.Proven a p2

values.name: modusTollens
values.comment: If a library implies that when p1 holds the p2 holds then you have proven (Not p2) by prove (Not p1)
values.type: RefinementProofs.Implies p1 p2 -> RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.Not p1) -> RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.Not p2)

values.name: not
values.comment: Used by library writers to create build a Not proof

values.name: or
values.comment: Used by library writers to create build a Or proof

values.name: since
values.comment: Synonym for "modus ponens". If a library implies that when p1 holds the p2 holds then you have proven p2 by prove p1
values.type: RefinementProofs.Implies p1 p2 -> RefinementProofs.Proven a p1 -> RefinementProofs.Proven a p2

values.name: sinceNot
values.comment: Synonym for "modus tollens". If a library implies that when p1 holds the p2 holds then you have proven (Not p2) by prove (Not p1)
values.type: RefinementProofs.Implies p1 p2 -> RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.Not p1) -> RefinementProofs.Proven a (RefinementProofs.Not p2)
